title: WA CFS Q2 2024: Credit Generation Declines While Deficits Rise in Q2'24
content:
Key Takeaways

  • In Q2'24, credit generation dropped 12.17% YoY to 517,980, with renewable diesel and ethanol as major contributors. Renewable diesel credits rose 25.87%, while biodiesel credits surged 84.33%. Ethanol credits fell 8.22% due to a 11.56% drop in volume.
  • Biodiesel volume increased 78.66% to 6.69 million gallons, while ethanol volume declined to 58.55 million gallons.
  • Q2'24 deficits rose 7.96% to 266,448, driven by higher ethanol (3.64%) and gasoline (13.97%) deficits, though diesel deficits dropped by 10.40% due to a 9.22% decline in diesel volume.
  • The Washington CFS draft, released on November 26 and open for comment through December 13, introduces book-and-claim accounting, expanded feedstock eligibility, and a 14-year credit period for methane capture projects.
author: Mansift Kaur
